openmeter/app/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing app.Adapter — persists installed apps and app-customer relationships, and holds the in-memory marketplace registry map[AppType]RegistryItem that every DB read (mapAppFromDB) consults to construct concrete app.App instances via the registered factory.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every mutating method
patterns.description: State-changing methods (CreateApp, UpdateApp, UninstallApp, EnsureCustomer) wrap Ent calls in transaction.Run + entutils.TransactingRepo. Read-only methods (ListApps, GetApp) use TransactingRepo alone.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context) (app.AppBase, error) { return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, repo *adapter) (app.AppBase, error) { ... }) })

patterns.name: TxCreator + TxUser triad
patterns.description: adapter implements Tx() via HijackTx+NewTxDriver, WithTx() via NewTxClientFromRawConfig, and Self(). All three are required for TransactingRepo to rebind to a ctx-carried transaction.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txClient := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txClient.Client(), registry: a.registry} }

patterns.name: mapAppFromDB delegates construction to factory
patterns.description: mapAppFromDB never type-switches on AppType; it calls registryItem.Factory.NewApp(ctx, appBase) to produce the concrete app.App, keeping the adapter type-agnostic.
patterns.example: app, err := registryItem.Factory.NewApp(ctx, appBase)

patterns.name: Soft-delete via DeletedAt
patterns.description: Apps and app-customer rows are never hard-deleted. UninstallApp sets DeletedAt on App; DeleteCustomer sets DeletedAt on AppCustomer. ListApps filters deleted unless IncludeDeleted.
patterns.example: query = query.Where(appdb.DeletedAtIsNil())

patterns.name: EnsureCustomer upsert with OnConflictColumns
patterns.description: AppCustomer rows are upserted on (namespace, app_id, customer_id); UpdateDeletedAt restores soft-deleted rows. The 'sql: no rows in result set' string is the known Ent issue #1821 and is silently ignored.
patterns.example: OnConflictColumns(appcustomerdb.FieldNamespace, appcustomerdb.FieldAppID, appcustomerdb.FieldCustomerID).UpdateDeletedAt().Exec(ctx)

key_file_guides.role: Struct, Config/New, Tx/WithTx/Self, compile-time assertion var _ app.Adapter = (*adapter)(nil).
key_file_guides.watch_for: registry map is value-initialised in New(); pointer receiver is essential so the registry is shared across all TransactingRepo-derived adapter copies.

key_file_guides.role: CreateApp, GetApp, ListApps, UpdateApp, UninstallApp, UpdateAppStatus, plus mappers mapAppBaseFromDB and mapAppFromDB.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateAppStatus uses TransactingRepo directly (no transaction.Run) — intentionally idempotent and not wrapped in a top-level transaction.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomerData (ListApps + per-app GetCustomerData), EnsureCustomer (upsert), DeleteCustomer (soft-delete).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListCustomerData calls GetCustomerData on each App which may trigger per-app-type Ent queries. Namespace derived from AppID or CustomerID; both nil => validation error.

key_file_guides.role: In-memory registry CRUD: RegisterMarketplaceListing, GetMarketplaceListing, ListMarketplaceListings, InstallMarketplaceListingWithAPIKey, InstallMarketplaceListing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegisterMarketplaceListing returns GenericConflictError on duplicate key. InstallMarketplaceListing type-asserts Factory to AppFactoryInstall (missing => GenericValidationError). OAuth2 methods return GenericNotImplementedError.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db directly in a method body that may run inside a caller-supplied transaction — always use entutils.TransactingRepo to rebind
Importing app-type-specific packages (appstripe, appsandbox, appcustominvoicing) here — construction is delegated to factory
Hard-deleting app or app-customer rows — the pattern is soft-delete via DeletedAt
Adding business logic beyond persistence — service-layer orchestration belongs in openmeter/app/service

decisions.decision: In-memory registry lives on the adapter struct (not a separate layer)
decisions.rationale: mapAppFromDB must consult the factory registry on every DB read; co-locating it with the Ent client keeps ListApps/GetApp atomic within one struct and avoids a second DI dependency.
OAuth2 app installation is not yet supported; stubbing keeps the adapter interface complete without forcing callers to handle nil adapters.

code_examples.scenario: Creating a new app inside a transaction with registry lookup

openmeter/app/httpdriver.purpose: v1 HTTP handler layer for the app domain — decodes requests, delegates to app.Service and billing.Service, encodes responses via httptransport.HandlerWithArgs. All domain-to-API mapping is centralised in mapper.go to keep handler files thin.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs three-closure pattern
patterns.description: Each endpoint returns a typed HandlerWithArgs: first closure decodes to Request, second invokes the service, third is a response encoder.
patterns.example: return httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decodeFn, operationFn, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Resp](http.StatusOK), httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("listApps"))...)

patterns.name: Request/Response/Handler type aliases per endpoint
patterns.description: Each endpoint defines <Op>Request, <Op>Response, <Op>Handler aliases so the router references the concrete handler type without importing httptransport.
patterns.example: type (ListAppsRequest = app.ListAppInput; ListAppsResponse = api.AppPaginatedResponse; ListAppsHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[ListAppsRequest, ListAppsResponse, ListAppsParams])

patterns.name: Namespace resolved via NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: Namespace is always resolved from ctx via h.resolveNamespace(ctx); never read from query params or body.
patterns.example: namespace, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx)

patterns.name: Type-switch discrimination for polymorphic app types
patterns.description: For discriminated-union API bodies (UpdateApp, CustomerAppData), call body.Discriminator() then the appropriate As<Type>(). Unknown types return GenericValidationError.
patterns.example: switch updateType { case string(app.AppTypeStripe): payload, _ := body.AsStripeAppReplaceUpdate(); ... default: return UpdateAppRequest{}, models.NewGenericValidationError(...) }

patterns.name: MapAppToAPI centralised in mapper.go
patterns.description: All domain-to-API mapping lives in mapper.go (MapAppToAPI, MapEventAppToAPI). MapAppToAPI type-asserts to the concrete provider type before a per-provider mapper.
patterns.example: case app.AppTypeStripe: stripeApp := item.(appstripe.App); app.FromStripeApp(mapStripeAppToAPI(stripeApp.Meta))

patterns.name: Customer deletion guard in decode closure
patterns.description: Customer existence and deletion checks (cus.IsDeleted()) are done in the decode closure to fail fast before any service call.
patterns.example: if cus != nil && cus.IsDeleted() { return ListCustomerDataRequest{}, models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError(...) }

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (AppHandler embeds endpoint groups), handler struct with injected services, New(), resolveNamespace helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: handler holds stripeAppService stripeapp.Service used only for GetSupplierContact during billing profile creation. Shared across requests — do not add per-request state fields.

key_file_guides.role: All domain-to-API conversions: MapAppToAPI, MapEventAppToAPI, ToAPIStripeCustomerAppData, fromAPIAppStripeCustomerData, per-type helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each new app type needs a case in MapAppToAPI and in toAPICustomerAppData. Missing cases return errors, not panics.

key_file_guides.role: ListApps, GetApp, UpdateApp, UninstallApp handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UninstallApp calls h.billingService.IsAppUsed before service.UninstallApp — this cross-domain check lives in the handler, not the service.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomerData, UpsertCustomerData, DeleteCustomerData. resolveCustomerApp falls back to billingService.GetCustomerApp when no appID is given.
key_file_guides.watch_for: toCustomerData discriminates app type then constructs domain CustomerData; requires concrete app package imports (appstripe, appsandbox, appcustominvoicing).

key_file_guides.role: ListMarketplaceListings, GetMarketplaceListing, MarketplaceAppAPIKeyInstall, MarketplaceAppInstall; post-install billing profile in createBillingProfile/makeStripeDefaultBillingApp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: makeStripeDefaultBillingApp only promotes Stripe to default if the current default profile uses AppTypeSandbox — idempotent for repeated installs.

anti_patterns: Inline domain-to-API mapping inside handler closures — all mapping belongs in mapper.go
Reading namespace from query params or path directly — always use h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Adding cross-domain pre-checks (billing, customer existence) inside the operation closure — these belong in the decode closure
Creating new handler struct fields for per-request state — the handler is shared across requests

decisions.decision: Handler holds both app.Service and billing.Service
decisions.rationale: Marketplace install optionally creates a billing profile as a side-effect; keeping both services in the handler avoids forcing billing logic into the app service layer.

decisions.decision: Type-switch in mapper.go rather than an interface method on app.App
decisions.rationale: App types are discriminated unions at the API boundary; concrete Go types are known at compile time here, making explicit switches safer than reflection or interface proliferation.

code_examples.scenario: Standard HandlerWithArgs endpoint wiring with namespace resolution and MapAppToAPI

openmeter/app/sandbox.purpose: Sandbox billing app — a no-op InvoicingApp for development and testing that simulates payment outcomes via invoice metadata, auto-provisions itself at namespace startup, and self-registers its MarketplaceListing at factory construction time.

patterns.name: App embeds Meta embeds AppBase
patterns.description: App embeds Meta (which embeds app.AppBase) plus injected services. Meta implements EventAppParser via FromEventAppData. Two-level embed separates base data from runtime behaviour.
patterns.example: type App struct { Meta; billingService billing.Service }; type Meta struct { app.AppBase }

patterns.name: Factory self-registers on construction
patterns.description: NewFactory calls config.AppService.RegisterMarketplaceListing inside the constructor; a registration failure returns an error so the listing is always registered before any app instance.
patterns.example: err := config.AppService.RegisterMarketplaceListing(ctx, app.RegistryItem{Listing: MarketplaceListing, Factory: fact})

patterns.name: Payment simulation via invoice metadata key
patterns.description: PostAdvanceStandardInvoiceHook reads invoice.Metadata[TargetPaymentStatusMetadataKey] to choose TriggerPaid/Failed/PaymentUncollectible/ActionRequired. Default is TriggerPaid.
patterns.example: override, ok := invoice.Metadata[TargetPaymentStatusMetadataKey]; if ok && override != "" { targetStatus = override }

patterns.name: AutoProvision idempotent helper
patterns.description: AutoProvision lists existing sandbox apps and creates one only if none exist; called at server startup for the default namespace.
patterns.example: if sandboxAppList.TotalCount == 0 { appBase, _ = input.AppService.CreateApp(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: MockableFactory for tests
patterns.description: MockableFactory wraps Factory with an overrideFactory. EnableMock(t) injects a MockApp; DisableMock() restores the real factory. Expectations set via On<Method>() and asserted in Reset(t).
patterns.example: mock := fact.EnableMock(t); mock.OnUpsertStandardInvoice(cb); defer fact.DisableMock()

patterns.name: No-op customer data methods
patterns.description: GetCustomerData, UpsertCustomerData, DeleteCustomerData all return nil/empty CustomerData{} — sandbox needs no external customer setup.
patterns.example: func (a App) GetCustomerData(...) (app.CustomerData, error) { return CustomerData{}, nil }

key_file_guides.role: App, Meta, CustomerData, Factory; Config/Validate; NewFactory; all billing.InvoicingApp methods; PostAdvanceStandardInvoiceHook.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FinalizeStandardInvoice calls GenerateInvoiceSequenceNumber with OM-SANDBOX prefix. UninstallApp is a no-op. InstallAppWithAPIKey creates the DB row then NewApp.

key_file_guides.role: MockApp (per-call stubs via mo.Option), mockAppInstance, MockableFactory, NewMockApp, NewMockableFactory.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MockApp.Reset calls AssertExpectations — unstaged expectations fail. mo.Option MustGet() panics if no expectation staged. Always Reset between cases.

key_file_guides.role: Singleton MarketplaceListing and Capability vars (CollectPayment, CalculateTax, InvoiceCustomer).
key_file_guides.watch_for: InstallMethods = [InstallMethodNoCredentials]. Factory also implements AppFactoryInstallWithAPIKey.

key_file_guides.role: AutoProvision — idempotent sandbox provisioning called at server startup after namespace creation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns the first sandbox app if multiple exist (non-deterministic ordering). Intended for single-sandbox-per-namespace use.

key_file_guides.role: ErrSimulatedPaymentFailure — a billing.ValidationError used for TargetPaymentStatusFailed simulations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Exported so tests assert on it with errors.Is.

anti_patterns: Storing actual payment or customer data in the sandbox — all customer data methods are intentionally no-ops
Registering the sandbox listing outside NewFactory — the constructor owns the registration lifecycle
Using MockApp without Reset(t) between test cases — unstaged mo.Option MustGet() panics
Firing billing triggers other than the four defined constants in PostAdvanceStandardInvoiceHook

decisions.decision: Payment outcome controlled by invoice metadata at hook time
decisions.rationale: Allows per-invoice test configuration without changing app config, making it easy to test different payment paths in one suite without reinstalling the sandbox.
Tests not needing mocking use the real factory path; EnableMock only overrides NewApp, so registration and uninstall logic stay tested through the real factory.

code_examples.scenario: NewFactory: validate config, construct factory, self-register listing

openmeter/app/service.purpose: Thin orchestration layer implementing app.Service — validates inputs, fires ServiceHooks[AppBase] around mutations inside transaction.Run, delegates all persistence to app.Adapter, and publishes domain events (AppCreate, AppUpdate, AppDelete) via eventbus.Publisher after successful writes.

patterns.name: Validate-then-delegate
patterns.description: Every public method calls input.Validate() first; on error it wraps with models.NewGenericValidationError and returns before any adapter/hook call.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all mutating operations
patterns.description: CreateApp, UpdateApp, UninstallApp, UpdateAppStatus wrap adapter calls in transaction.Run. Hook firing and event publishing happen inside the closure so a hook error atomically rolls back the DB write.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (app.App, error) { /* PreUpdate -> UpdateApp -> PostUpdate -> Publish */ })

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[AppBase] fired inside transaction closure
patterns.description: Pre* hooks fire before the adapter call, Post* after. CreateApp has PostCreate; UpdateApp has Pre+PostUpdate; UninstallApp has Pre+PostDelete. A hook error rolls back atomically.
patterns.example: s.hooks.PreDelete(ctx, &existingBase) -> adapter.UninstallApp -> s.hooks.PostDelete

patterns.name: Pre-fetch existing app before transaction for hook payload
patterns.description: UpdateApp and UninstallApp fetch the existing app via adapter.GetApp before opening the transaction, providing the hook's 'before' snapshot without a lock.
patterns.example: existingApp, err := s.adapter.GetApp(ctx, input.AppID)  // before transaction.Run

patterns.name: Domain event published inside transaction closure
patterns.description: CreateApp/UpdateApp/UpdateAppStatus/UninstallApp publish via s.publisher.Publish inside the transaction closure.
patterns.example: event := app.NewAppCreateEvent(ctx, appBase); if err := s.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { return app.AppBase{}, err }

patterns.name: Config struct with Validate() + per-file compile-time assertions
patterns.description: Service built via New(Config); Config.Validate() rejects nil deps. Each file declares var _ app.AppService = (*Service)(nil).
patterns.example: var _ app.AppService = (*Service)(nil) // in app.go, customer.go, marketplace.go

key_file_guides.role: Service struct, Config/Validate, New(), RegisterHooks. Holds adapter, publisher, hooks (ServiceHookRegistry[AppBase]).
key_file_guides.watch_for: No requestValidators field — pre-mutation cross-domain guards are out of scope for the app domain.

key_file_guides.role: CreateApp, GetApp, UpdateApp, ListApps, UninstallApp, UpdateAppStatus — validation, hook fan-out inside transaction.Run, event publishing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: UpdateApp pre-fetches the existing app before the transaction. If AppConfigUpdate is provided, it calls UpdateAppConfig then re-fetches via GetApp inside the same transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for ServiceHooks; failing PostCreate rolls back the DB write.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses apptestutils.NewTestEnv with RegisterSandboxFactory:true to get GetApp/UpdateApp/UninstallApp working without a billing service.

key_file_guides.role: ListCustomerData, EnsureCustomer, DeleteCustomer — thin pass-throughs to adapter, no event publishing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: No events emitted for customer data mutations — intentional; customer data changes are app-internal.

key_file_guides.role: RegisterMarketplaceListing, GetMarketplaceListing, ListMarketplaceListings, Install* and OAuth2 methods — pass-throughs with input validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegisterMarketplaceListing does NOT emit an event — it mutates the in-memory registry on the adapter and is called at startup, not per-request.

anti_patterns: Bypassing input.Validate() before adapter calls — validation must happen at the service layer
Adding Ent queries directly to service methods — all persistence must go through app.Adapter
Publishing events inside the adapter — event publishing is the service layer's responsibility
Registering hooks from inside a domain package constructor — always register from app/common to avoid import cycles

decisions.decision: Service delegates all persistence to app.Adapter without additional business logic
decisions.rationale: Business logic lives in concrete app implementations (appsandbox, appstripe) and their factories; the service is purely an orchestration and validation boundary.

decisions.decision: Events published inside transaction.Run, not after commit
decisions.rationale: Watermill Kafka publish is not transactional with Postgres; publishing inside transaction.Run means a failing publish rolls back the write, preventing phantom events on rollback.

code_examples.scenario: UpdateApp: validate, pre-fetch, transact, hook, re-fetch, post-hook, publish

openmeter/app/testutils.purpose: Fully wired app domain test environment backed by a real PostgreSQL database — provides an Env struct with AppService and Adapter pre-wired, helpers for installing/fetching sandbox apps, and a minimal no-op sandbox factory for tests needing app lifecycle without billing logic.

patterns.name: NewTestEnv wires the full domain stack against a real DB
patterns.description: NewTestEnv calls testutils.InitPostgresDB, constructs appadapter and appservice, optionally registers a Sandbox factory, and returns Env. Each test gets an isolated DB via pgtestdb.
patterns.example: env := testutils.NewTestEnv(t, NewEnvConfig{RegisterSandboxFactory: true}); defer env.Close(t)

patterns.name: Conditional Sandbox factory registration
patterns.description: With BillingService, the full appsandbox.Factory registers (billing logic). With RegisterSandboxFactory but no BillingService, a minimalSandboxFactory (no-op) registers — for CRUD-only tests.
patterns.example: NewEnvConfig{BillingService: billingSvc} vs NewEnvConfig{RegisterSandboxFactory: true}

patterns.name: DBSchemaMigrate for schema setup
patterns.description: Tests needing Ent schema must call env.DBSchemaMigrate(t), which runs Schema.Create on the test DB client.
patterns.example: env.DBSchemaMigrate(t)

patterns.name: sync.Once in Close for safe cleanup
patterns.description: Env.Close wraps teardown in sync.Once so it is safe from multiple defer sites and t.Cleanup; closes both the Ent and PG drivers.
patterns.example: defer env.Close(t)

patterns.name: InstallSandboxApp and MustGetApp helpers
patterns.description: Package-level helpers that assert success and fail the test on error, removing boilerplate.
patterns.example: installedApp := testutils.InstallSandboxApp(t, env, namespace)

key_file_guides.role: Env struct, NewTestEnv, DBSchemaMigrate, Close, minimalSandboxFactory, minimalSandboxApp, InstallSandboxApp, MustGetApp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: minimalSandboxFactory.NewApp returns a minimalSandboxApp implementing app.App but not billing.InvoicingApp — tests needing invoice flows must pass a real billing.Service. Do not import app/common here.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from this testutils package — build dependencies must come from underlying constructors to avoid import cycles
Calling env.DBSchemaMigrate without an initialised DB — the db field must be set (InitPostgresDB does this in NewTestEnv)
Using minimalSandboxFactory in tests that exercise billing invoice flows — use NewEnvConfig{BillingService: ...}
Sharing a single Env across multiple test functions — each test should call NewTestEnv for an isolated database

decisions.decision: minimalSandboxFactory registered separately from full appsandbox.Factory
decisions.rationale: Most app-domain tests (CRUD, hooks, lifecycle) do not exercise billing invoice paths; the minimal factory avoids requiring a fully wired billing service, reducing setup complexity.

code_examples.scenario: Setting up an app test environment and installing a sandbox app
